title: The People See the End of Wapol
description: On pages 11–13, this part focuses on the people see the end of wapol, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The People See the End of Wapol. Luffy defeats Wapol and destroys the symbol of his selfish rule, clearing the way for Drum’s rebirth. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Gahhhh!!!!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: AaaG how HHH!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee he

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: It's his own damn fault for running around while still injured.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Dalton... you don't happen to know where the key to this castle's armour is,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Do you...?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: The summary? Why would you even want the key to that?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What does it matter to you?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Wapol's always kept that key with him at all times,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: So I would imagine it's disappeared into the sky along with him...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Is that so?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Well, that's trouble- some...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Doc- Press...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: About the treatment fee for me and my crewmates...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Can I get you to waive it off? Oh, and allow us to leave right away as well, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hmm?.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Is that supposed to be some sort of bad joke? I'll not only be taking all the money you have but also whatever else you've got on your ship as treatment costs.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Also, you're not leaving anywhere for another 2 whole days, missy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Listen to the doctor, Nami! We have to make sure you're back to full health before-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's fine. I don't feel like I'm on the verge of death anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's not medically- sound proof that you're healthy now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The key to the armour...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You need this, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What!? How in the world!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Is that the real key? How did you get it?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I swiped it.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: forcing me to accept your ridiculous demands...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: You've sure got some guts for a little girl.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: further, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time, full-time,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: ...Fine, I'll waive off your treatment bills.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Clunk...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: But! I won't accept your second condition.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I can't allow it as a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What, no! Hey, give that key back then!

summary: This part highlights the people see the end of wapol and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
